sanskrit:
ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक्।
ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्चितैः॥
transliteration: ṛṣibhir bahudhā gītaṃ chandobhir vividhaiḥ pṛthak | brahma-sūtra-padaiś caiva hetumadbhir viniścitaiḥ ||
literal_meaning: By sages / in many ways / sung / with Vedic metres / various / separately / Vedānta-sūtra / aphorisms / and / indeed / with reasoning / ascertained
contextual_meaning: The same truth about the field (body-mind) and its knower (consciousness) has been repeatedly confirmed by multiple authoritative voices, using rigorous logic and diverse symbolic languages.
psychological_state: A moment of cognitive flexibility—Arjuna is invited to hold his experience lightly, knowing that many valid descriptions of mind-body-consciousness exist and have been cross-validated by sages across traditions.
trigger_scenario: A client in therapy realizes that CBT, IFS, somatic experiencing and Buddhist mindfulness are all describing the same inner landscape with different vocabularies; instead of defending one model they feel relief that no single story owns the territory.
anchor_text: The same truth is sung by sages in many ways.
